channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Reactions from the hometown of man who attacked the synagogue in Michigan
item.description: The man who attacked a synagogue in Michigan lost family members in an Israeli strike in Lebanon. Relatives and neighbors in his hometown share their views on his actions.
item.content:encoded: The man who attacked a synagogue in Michigan lost family members in an Israeli strike in Lebanon. Relatives and neighbors in his hometown share their views on his actions.
item.dc:creator: Hadeel Al-Shalchi

item.title: Russian strike on Kyiv region kills 4 and wounds 15, with peace talks stalled
item.description: The strikes comes after the United States paused ceasefire talks between Russia and Ukraine due to the war with Iran.
item.content:encoded:
The strikes comes after the United States paused ceasefire talks between Russia and Ukraine due to the war with Iran.

(Image credit: Efrem Lukatsky)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Brazil's ex-President Bolsonaro is in intensive care with pneumonia, hospital says
item.description: One of Bolsonaro's doctors described the former Brazilian president's medical condition as "serious."
item.content:encoded:
One of Bolsonaro's doctors described the former Brazilian president's medical condition as "serious."

(Image credit: Eraldo Peres)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: How the conflict in Iran is affecting global markets
item.description: Escalation of the Iran conflict is sending shockwaves through global markets — driving up oil, fuel, and commodity prices, stoking inflation and recession risks worldwide. We hear from three NPR reporters in Europe, Asia and Russia.
item.content:encoded: Escalation of the Iran conflict is sending shockwaves through global markets — driving up oil, fuel, and commodity prices, stoking inflation and recession risks worldwide. We hear from three NPR reporters in Europe, Asia and Russia.
item.dc:creator: Charles Maynes

item.title: U.S. Embassy in Baghdad again urges Americans to leave Iraq as Trump touts strikes on Iran
item.description: In a post on Truth Social, President Trump claimed the U.S. had "destroyed 100% of Iran's Military capability." The U.S. Embassy in Baghdad, meanwhile, urged Americans to "leave Iraq immediately."
item.content:encoded:
In a post on Truth Social, President Trump claimed the U.S. had "destroyed 100% of Iran's Military capability." The U.S. Embassy in Baghdad, meanwhile, urged Americans to "leave Iraq immediately."

(Image credit: Jalaa Marey)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: These are the casualties and cost of the war in Iran 2 weeks into the conflict
item.description: The war in Iran has already cost the U.S. billions of dollars. Here's the impact by the numbers.
item.content:encoded:
The war in Iran has already cost the U.S. billions of dollars. Here's the impact by the numbers.

(Image credit: Majid Saeedi)
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger

item.title: Ukrainian President Zelenskyy concerned as U.S. suspends Russia sanctions
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy meets French President Emmanuel Macron in Paris to increase pressure on Russia, as the U.S. suspends oil sanctions.
item.content:encoded: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy meets French President Emmanuel Macron in Paris to increase pressure on Russia, as the U.S. suspends oil sanctions.
item.dc:creator: Eleanor Beardsley

item.title: Some Gen Z Americans can't stop 'Chinamaxxing'
item.description: Despite rising tensions between the world's two largest economies, a growing number of young Americans are becoming captivated by China, as seen in the online trend "Chinamaxxing."
item.content:encoded:
Despite rising tensions between the world's two largest economies, a growing number of young Americans are becoming captivated by China, as seen in the online trend "Chinamaxxing."

(Image credit: Adek Berry)
item.dc:creator: Ashish Valentine

item.title: FBI investigates attacks in Michigan and Virginia. And, Senate passes housing bill
item.description: The FBI is investigating two separate attacks, one in Michigan and the other in Virginia, that happened yesterday. And, the Senate has passed the largest housing bill in decades.
item.content:encoded:
The FBI is investigating two separate attacks, one in Michigan and the other in Virginia, that happened yesterday. And, the Senate has passed the largest housing bill in decades.

(Image credit: Emily Elconin)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Israel launches airstrikes in Beirut, displacing thousands of people
item.description: Israel has carried out air strikes in central Beirut for the first time since the latest conflict began, displacing hundreds of thousands of people.
item.content:encoded: Israel has carried out air strikes in central Beirut for the first time since the latest conflict began, displacing hundreds of thousands of people.
item.dc:creator: Hadeel Al-Shalchi
